data.title: Trade Tensions and AI Risks Are Reshaping the Global Market Outlook for the Next Two Years
data.description:
The World Economic Forum's latest Global Risks Report sends a clear message to global markets as 2026 is in its early stages. Economic tension between major powers is now the top concern for business leaders worldwide. As a result, investors are facing a market shaped less by growth cycles and more by conflict over trade, supply chains, and capital access. Nearly half of the surveyed executives expect unstable conditions over the next two years.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, only 1% expect calm. This outlook matters for stocks, bonds, and currencies because rising friction often leads to slower trade, higher costs, and weaker global demand. In simple terms, when countries use tariffs and rules as tools, markets tend to price in risk rather than growth.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: SEALSQ (LAES) Eyes Quobly Acquisition in Push for Quantum-Safe Tech
data.description:
SEALSQ (LAES), a post‑quantum cybersecurity and digital identity solutions provider, has begun formal discussions to acquire Quobly, a French startup developing next‑generation quantum computing technologies. The potential deal might help strengthen the company's position in the fast‑evolving quantum‑safe ecosystem.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Quobly, formerly known as Siquance, builds silicon quantum chips that can scale and work with today's semiconductor factories. Importantly, its technology fits well with SEALSQ's goal of creating secure hardware and software that can resist future quantum-powered cyber threats.

If completed, the acquisition would give SEALSQ access to Quobly's engineering talent, intellectual property, and research partnerships across Europe.

Why This Acquisition Matters 

Quantum computing is advancing quickly, and with it comes the risk that future quantum machines could break today's encryption standards. Companies like SEALSQ are racing to build "quantum‑safe" technologies before that becomes a reality.

Quobly's acquisition will help SEALSQ move faster on quantum‑secure chip development, grow its footprint in Europe's deep‑tech sector, and strengthen its offerings for government, defense, and enterprise customers preparing for post-quantum security needs.

SEALSQ Reaffirms Robust 2026 Growth Outlook

Recently, SEALSQ reported preliminary 2025 revenue of $18 million, up 66% from last year, with support from stronger demand for legacy products and added revenue from its IC'ALPS acquisition.

The company posted $8 million in Q4 revenue and ended the year with over $425 million in cash to support its quantum‑security expansion.

Looking ahead, SEALSQ reaffirmed its outlook for 2026 revenue to grow between 50% to 100%, driven by a full year of IC'ALPS contributions, new post‑quantum chips, and early Quantix Edge revenue. It also noted a business pipeline of more than $200 million in potential deals through 2028.

Is LAES a Strong Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, LAES stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on a single Buy rating assigned by Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Troy Jensen in the past three months. Jensen's price target of $7 on SEALSQ stock implies 62.41% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Palantir and CrowdStrike: Daniel Ives Reveals His Top AI Stocks for 2026
data.description:
AI has been generating hype and headlines over the past three years, since OpenAI launched the generative AI revolution with the release of ChatGPT. Since then, AI stocks have been driving the market's bull run, and fueling gains in the tech sector – gains that, in some cases, have been measured in the trillions of dollars.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wedbush's Daniel Ives has been looking into the sector, and putting his finger on the pulse of leading stocks at all scales. The analyst describes AI as a new Industrial Revolution, with potential to reshape the economy and the larger world for decades to come.

Describing what's likely to happen in 2026, Ives writes, "Heading into 2026 investors are both excited and nervous about the AI Revolution. On one hand this is a 4th Industrial Revolution taking shape with the US leading China on tech for the first in 30 years…while on the other hand the trillions of dollars needed to reach the heights of enterprise/consumer AI Revolution have caused nervousness among investors. We believe both sides can be right at the same time and it ultimately speaks to 2026 being an inflection point year for the AI Revolution."

The 5-star analyst, rated by TipRanks among the top 4% of Wall Street's stock experts, has followed this by tagging Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) and CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) as two of his top AI stocks for 2026, shares that should bring solid gains for investors. Let's take a closer look at them, and find out how Ives' stance compares to the overall Wall Street view.

Palantir Technologies

The first of Ives' picks that we'll look at is Palantir, one of the better-known names in the AI industry. The company was founded in 2003, and offers its users a set of platforms and tools designed to make AI intuitive at the user end, by matching up the speed of AI computing power with the flexibility of human operators. Following this approach, Palantir has become a powerful mega-cap player in the field, with a ~$427 billion market cap valuation and a reputation as a leader in AI-powered data analytic services that are both innovative and high-end.

Palantir has achieved this by integrating AI tech directly into the user interface of its platform, a key feature that allows human system users to interact directly with the AI, entering data and queries in natural language and receiving replies the same way. This cuts out the need for complex computer or coding languages, and makes Palantir's products accessible with a bare minimum of training.

In short, what Palantir does best is to make AI-powered data analysis readily available. The company's products have proven widely popular and applicable at many levels, across the public and private sectors.

A couple of the company's recent announcements show the range of its versatility: this month, Palantir announced a joint effort with Teton Ridge to bring real-time AI and computer vision to the rodeo niche, in a move that will enhance the sport's experience for athletes, partners, and fans; and Palantir has announced a partnership with the US Navy, a $448 million plan to bring Palantir's AI Platform into the Navy's shipbuilding sector.

Palantir's continuing fast growth was visible in its last earnings reports, which covered 3Q25. The company reported 63% year-over-year growth at the top line, which reached $1.18 billion and exceeded expectations by $89.5 million. The company had a bottom line, non-GAAP EPS of 21 cents, a figure that was 4 cents per share ahead of the forecasts, and it finished Q3 with deep pockets, $6.4 billion in cash and other liquid assets.

Palantir's fast growth in recent years—the stock is up more than 2,400% in the last three years, and 171% over the past 12 months—has caused investors to worry about overvaluation, but Daniel Ives takes a bullish stance. For Ives, the key point here is the strong demand for the services that Palantir has to offer. He writes of the company, "PLTR continues to see unprecedented demand for AIP across both commercial and government landscapes by taking AI solutions that solve meaningful problems across organizations at enterprise scale driving new customer conversions and existing deal expansions. With the company making strategic moves to remain at the forefront of AI, we believe that PLTR has a golden path to become a trillion-dollar market cap company and will grow into its valuation as Karp and Co. remain one of the largest players in the AI Revolution."

Ives quantifies his stance with an Outperform (Buy) rating, and a $230 price target that suggests room for a 28.5% gain for the shares over the next 12 months. (To watch Ives' track record, click here)

This view is the bullish case. Wall Street is somewhat more cautious. PLTR shares have a Hold consensus rating, based on 17 recent analyst reviews that include 5 to Buy, 10 to Hold, and 2 to Sell. Palantir's stock is selling for $178.96, and its $192.88 average target price implies a one-year gain of 8%. (See PLTR stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/14/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Slip Amid Weak Bank Earnings, PPI Data Ahead
data.description:
U.S. stock futures slipped early Wednesday as traders reacted to weak earnings from banking giant JPMorgan (JPM). Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.16%, 0.15%, and 0.21%, respectively, at 3:35 a.m. EST on January 14.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed lower, dragged down by banking stocks. The S&P 500 fell 0.19%, the Dow dropped 0.80%, and the Nasdaq Composite slipped 0.10%. Shares of JPMorgan Chase dropped over 4% as its Q4 investment banking fees came in below expectations. Goldman Sachs (GS) and Bank of America (BAC) stocks also fell.

In December, the Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose 2.7% from a year earlier, matching forecasts and November's rate. Core CPI, excluding food and energy, climbed 2.6% year-over-year, below expectations but flat from last month. Inflation has cooled lately but remains above the Federal Reserve's 2% target.

On the economic front, traders will closely watch today's Producer Price Index (PPI) data for December and the retail sales report.

On the earnings front, notable companies reporting today include Bank of America (BAC), Citigroup (C), Wells Fargo (WFC), and Infosys (INFY).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.17%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $60.69 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $4,624 per ounce on Wednesday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today as markets focus on a meeting between U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Greenlandic and Danish officials to discuss President Trump's ongoing interest in acquiring the Danish territory of Greenland. Denmark and Greenland insist the mineral-rich Arctic island is not for sale, despite Trump's suggestions of using military force.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets traded mixed on January 14 due to the ongoing geopolitical unrest in Iran.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index added 0.56%. In China, the Shanghai Composite dropped 0.31%, while the Shenzhen Component gained 0.79%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei rose 1.48%, and the Topix surged 1.26%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Why Super Micro Stock (SMCI) Is Sliding — and What Goldman Sachs Sees Next
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (SMCI) has been one of the standout names tied to the AI server boom, supplying hardware used across fast-growing data center and cloud markets. However, the stock has come under pressure lately. The stock fell about 5% on Wednesday and continued to trade lower in today's pre-market action after Goldman Sachs initiated coverage with a Sell rating and assigned a Street-low price target of $26, raising doubts about Super Micro's profit outlook.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Profitability Remains the Key Concern

Top Goldman analyst Katherine Murphy said Super Micro should remain a leader in AI servers in the medium term, especially with tier-2 cloud and neocloud customers. Still, she warned that strong demand alone may not be enough to support earnings growth.

The main concern remains margins. Murphy said Super Micro continues to win business through large deals that weigh on profitability, making future earnings harder to forecast. She believes that these deals help drive revenue but limit upside to margins.

At the same time, increasing competition remains a matter of concern. Murphy pointed to growing pressure from both established EMs and lower-cost ODMs, which are pushing pricing lower across the AI server market and leaving little room for margin improvement.

Murphy also pointed to Super Micro's push into enterprise and sovereign customers. While this could support growth over time, the added investment is raising costs today, with no clear timeline for when returns will improve.

These factors have already weighed on the company's earnings estimates. Even so, Murphy believes margins could still face further pressure, limiting confidence in Super Micro's forward outlook.

Is SMCI Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold Now? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on SMCI stock based on five Buys, five Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average SMCI price target of $44.82 per share implies 56.71% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: QBTS, RGTI, IONQ – Mizuho Highlights Key Advances in Quantum Computing for 2026
data.description:
Mizuho Securities top analyst Vijay Rakesh highlighted key advances in quantum computing for 2026. He reiterated Buy ratings on three pure-play quantum firms: D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), Rigetti Computing (RGTI), and IonQ Inc. (IONQ).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

He pointed out some major updates such as RGTI's Cepheus-1-108Q – its first 100+ qubit system arriving in Q1 2026, QBTS' acquisition of Quantum Circuits, and IONQ's strategic expansion through KISTI in South Korea and QuantumBasel collaboration in Switzerland.

Rakesh is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #11 out of 12,040 analysts tracked. He has a 68% success rate and an impressive average return per rating of 44.40%.

QBTS Stock: Progress in Gate-Based Quantum Annealing

Rakesh noted that QBTS' $550 million acquisition of Quantum Circuits, a company specializing in superconducting quantum computers, could speed up its gate-based systems by about 3 years. This deal makes QBTS the first big quantum company with both annealing and gate-model superconducting quantum tech.

He also thinks that QBTS may qualify for more NQI funding (a U.S. government program), with this gate-based system in development. D-Wave recently demoed the first on-chip cryogenic control of gate-model qubits. This breakthrough cuts down cooling parts without losing accuracy and helping to scale up qubits faster.

Rakesh has a $46 price target on QBTS, implying 59.6% upside potential from current levels.

RGTI Stock: Long-Term Roadmap Intact with 108Q System

Rakesh pointed out that RGTI updated the timeline for its 108-qubit system, Cepheus-1-108Q. It is now slated for general availability at the end of Q1 2026 (previously end of 2025). The delay was due to challenges with the "tunable couplers," and extra time needed to test and hit 99% two-qubit gate fidelity.

RGTI also shared improved goals of 99.7% fidelity for the 9-qubit system and 99.6% for the 36-qubit system. Despite the short-term delay, Rakesh believes Rigetti's long-term plan remains on track: a 1,000+ qubit system with 99.8% two-qubit fidelity by end of 2027.

Rakesh maintained his $50 price target on RGTI, implying a massive 104.3% upside potential from current levels.

IONQ Stock: Lucrative Partnerships Cement Long-Term Success

Rakesh noted that IONQ solidified its 2026 pipeline via a 100-qubit Tempo delivery to KISTI (South Korea) and QuantumBasel expansion (Switzerland), lifting the latter's value to over $60 million through 2029. These deals underscore IONQ's trapped-ion leadership in scalability and reliability, with Tempo's algorithmic qubits enabling practical advantage sooner than rivals.

Quantitatively, the roughly 2x value increase in QuantumBasel reflects rising demand, while strategically, international footprints reduce U.S.-centric risks and validate revenue scalability. IonQ also plans to launch a 256-qubit system by 2026 and expand into quantum networking.

Rakesh kept his $90 price target on IONQ, which suggests about 84% upside potential from current levels.

Concluding Thoughts

Rakesh believes that quantum computing is poised for major breakthroughs in 2026 apart from those mentioned above. Catalysts like 30%-40% market growth, $2.7 billion in NQI funding through 2029, cloud service expansions by Amazon Web Services (AMZN)/ Microsoft (MSFT) Azure/Alphabet (GOOGL), IBM's (IBM) predicted quantum advantage, Google's Willow, and Quantinuum's Helios/Apollo by 2029 are all set to bolster the sector this year.

Based on TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, QBTS and IONQ have a Strong Buy consensus rating, with IONQ offering a higher upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.description:
Shares in cybersecurity companies such as Palo Alto Networks (PANW), Fortinet (FTNT), and chipmaker Broadcom (AVGO) — which owns VMware — dropped early Wednesday. This came after a Reuters report that Chinese authorities had told domestic firms to steer clear of around 12 U.S. and Israeli software vendors on national security grounds.

data.title: Hollywood Faces AI Cloning Risk as Matthew McConaughey Tests a Trademark Strategy
data.description:
The rise of AI is creating new challenges across the entertainment industry, especially around control of image and voice. Studios, actors, and platforms are still working through how far AI use should go and who owns the result. As fake videos and cloned voices spread online, legal tools have struggled to keep pace. Against that backdrop, actor Matthew McConaughey is testing a new way to protect himself. This is according to a report from The Wall Street Journal.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Instead of waiting for new laws, McConaughey is turning to trademark rules that already exist. His goal is to create clear ownership around his voice and likeness before misuse becomes a larger problem. The move reflects a wider shift in Hollywood toward prevention rather than reaction.

Using trademarks to protect identity

McConaughey has secured approval from the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office for several trademarks tied directly to him. These include short video clips of him speaking or smiling, along with audio of his well-known line – 'Alright, alright, alright…' from the film 'Dazed and Confused'. The intent is simple. If someone uses his voice or image without consent, his team can act quickly in federal court.

Normally, actors rely on state-level publicity rights to stop misuse. Those laws can be slow and vary by location. Trademarks, by contrast, fall under federal law and can be enforced more broadly.

However, legal experts note that this approach is untested. Courts may still debate whether a person can function as a brand under trademark rules. Still, the threat of a federal claim alone could deter casual misuse and push platforms to respond faster.

A signal to AI firms and investors

At the same time, McConaughey is not rejecting AI outright. He recently partnered with ElevenLabs to produce a Spanish version of his newsletter using AI voice tools. He is also an investor in the company. That detail helps clarify the message. AI use is acceptable when it is licensed and approved.

More broadly, Hollywood unions and major studios continue to push for federal laws that limit AI replicas without permission. A bill was introduced in 2024, but it has not advanced since. Until clearer rules are in place, efforts like McConaughey's show how individuals are using existing law to fill the gap.

Using TipRanks' Comparison Tool, we gathered a group of notable entertainment stocks that are being shaped by the rise of AI. The tool helps investors view companies side by side and better understand how the broader entertainment industry is adapting to this stage of AI adoption.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Amazon Stock (AMZN) Slips as It Prepares to Battle Italian Watchdog in Court
data.description:
Amazon's (AMZN) shares stayed in the red early Wednesday as the American retail giant noted its intention to appeal the reduced $876.3 million antitrust fine from Italian authorities.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

AGCM, Italy's competition authority, on Monday announced that it has trimmed its earlier €1.13 billion (currently about $1.32 billion) fine on the e-commerce giant after an Italian court in September permitted the fine to be reduced. The Italian watchdog had imposed the fine on Amazon in 2021, saying the Washington-based company restricted market competition in the Southern European country's e-commerce logistics services market, thereby abusing its dominant market position.

While the court backed key findings by AGCM, it contended that the Italian watchdog did not provide sufficient defense for including a discretionary 50% surcharge in the previous fine. Italian newspaper MF has reported that the competition watchdog also plans to appeal the ruling enforcing the fine cut.

Amazon Faces Tax Probe in Italy

Meanwhile, Amazon is also facing an alleged $1.3 billion tax probe in Italy in connection with an investigation launched in 2021 by Italian authorities. In November, Italian police raided two of the e-commerce company's sites in the country as part of an investigation into suspected smuggling of Chinese goods into the country.

In a separate case, tech rival Meta (META) is also under the radar of Italian authorities as part of a probe into suspected abuse of its dominant market position. The AGCM recently forced the U.S. tech giant to reverse plans to exclude rival AI chatbots from its messaging platform WhatsApp.

Is Amazon a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

On Wall Street, Amazon's shares continue to carry a Strong Buy consensus rating from analysts based on 46 Buys and one Hold issued over the past three months.

In addition, the average AMZN price target of $295.05 suggests about 22% growth potential from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Why This Top Analyst Thinks Amazon's Ad Revenue Can Double by 2030
data.description:
Top TD Cowen analyst John Blackledge believes Amazon's (AMZN) advertising business is entering a powerful new phase. After reviewing his firm's latest U.S. ad buyer survey, he raised his price target on Amazon to $315 from $300 and reiterated a Buy rating. The new price target implies a 30% upside from current levels.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What the Ad Buyer Survey Showed

According to the survey results, more than 60% of advertisers already using Amazon said they expect to increase spending in 2026, which Blackledge sees as a clear sign that Amazon will gain ad share over time.

The five-star analyst also noted that advertisers are increasingly using Amazon's data to reach shoppers both on Amazon and across the wider internet. He expects this part of the business to continue taking share from competing ad platforms.

Another factor the analyst highlighted is Prime Video advertising. By adding ads to Prime Video, Amazon is giving brands access to large audiences through video, while keeping them inside Amazon's ecosystem.

Blackledge also sees artificial intelligence playing an important role in helping advertisers build and run more effective ads. He said GenAI tools are helping advertisers create and optimize ads more easily, leading to better results and supporting higher spending over time.

Putting all of this together, Blackledge estimates Amazon's advertising revenue can grow from about $68 billion in 2025 to nearly $142 billion by 2030, equal to a 16% yearly growth rate. He also expects Amazon's share of global digital advertising outside China to rise steadily over that period.

Is Amazon a Buy, Sell, or Hold?  

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on AMZN stock based on 46 Buys and one Hold assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AMZN price target of $295.05 per share implies 21.62% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
